name: Verify and Falsify
content:
Review recent work with fresh context, try to falsify it, and fix clear in-scope issues as you find them.

Assume I have just completed work and want an objective verification-and-repair pass, ideally from a new context window or outside model.

## Goals

- Explain what was developed in plain English.
- Verify the implementation against the intended criteria, spec, or requested behavior.
- Look for weaknesses, mismatches, omissions, broken assumptions, regressions, rule violations, downstream issues, and edge cases.
- Make focused fixes when the correct fix is clear, safe, and within scope.
- Report what was verified, fixed, and still risky.

## Review and Repair Rules

Analyze the recent changes and related spec or intended behavior objectively. Look for inconsistencies between implementation and criteria, plus broken tests, invalid JSON/Markdown, stale command descriptions, missing linked documentation, acceptance-criteria gaps, or obvious downstream breakage.

When a fix is directly supported by context, implement it immediately instead of only reporting it. Keep fixes minimal, targeted, and consistent with the existing codebase or artifact style. Do not rewrite unrelated areas or expand scope because of adjacent improvements.

If an issue is real but cannot be fixed safely from available context, report it clearly rather than guessing. Stop and report instead of making fixes that require product decisions, broad refactors, destructive operations, credentials, external access, or unclear intent.

Run or recommend the most relevant verification after fixes when possible. Preserve user changes and unrelated work.

## Final Response

Start with what you verified and what you fixed. Then explain the recent work in plain English, list fixes grouped by file or concern, list verification performed and results, and list remaining issues or follow-up only if they still exist. Keep the summary direct and evidence-based.

--- Secondary ---

Include quality-control checks for oversized files, large diffs, or other signs that changes may have introduced hidden problems when useful. If useful, frame the review as if performed by an outside model with no prior attachment to the work.
description: Fresh-context verification and repair pass - objectively review recent work, fix clear issues as they are found, and summarize what changed.
